alternative_expected_tool_calls.reason: Entity name "Garage Door" is unambiguous in this inventory; domain hint is not required for correct resolution.

metadata.notes: Step 1: HassTurnOn × cover (= open). Garage door, binary (no position), device_class: garage. HassTurnOn = open for covers.

alternative_expected_tool_calls.reason: HassSetPosition(position=0) is equivalent to HassTurnOff for position-capable blinds.
Entity name "Living Room Blinds" is unambiguous in this inventory; domain hint is not required for correct resolution.

metadata.notes: Retargeted from cover.office_blinds (not in medium) to cover.living_room_blinds.

utterance: are the living room blinds open

alternative_expected_tool_calls.reason: Entity name "Living Room Blinds" is unambiguous in this inventory; domain hint is not required for correct resolution.

metadata.notes: Step 1: HassGetState × cover. State is open. Note: HassGetState returns open/closed, NOT position percentage. This is the tool limitation targeted by a cross_domain probe case.

utterance: set the living room blinds to 40 percent

metadata.notes: Retargeted from cover.office_blinds (not in medium) to cover.living_room_blinds.

utterance: raise the standing desk to 75 percent

metadata.notes: Step 2: Standing desk subtype — unusual domain mapping (desk height control via cover domain). Tests 'cover = blinds' bias. Entity has supported_features=12 (SET_POSITION+STOP, no open/close). HassSetPosition is the correct tool.
Step 5: Area-scoped cover command. Living Room has cover.living_room_blinds (venetian, open) and cover.living_room_curtains (curtain, open). Model should use area+domain targeting.
